About.Name: Arjun Sapkota
About.AboutText: Hi, I am Arjun Sapkota. I am an electrical engineering student with a strong interest in electronics, circuits, and power systems. Currently, I am pursuing my undergraduate degree at Seattle Pacific University. I enjoy learning how electrical systems work and solving technical problems related to engineering. I like working on practical projects and improving my skills in areas such as circuit analysis, programming, and electrical design. I am always interested in learning new technologies and gaining hands-on experience through projects and teamwork. Outside of my classes, I try to expand my knowledge by exploring different topics in engineering and technology. I believe that continuous learning and curiosity are important for becoming a good engineer.

Experience.ExperienceText: I am currently pursuing a degree in Electrical Engineering, developing both theoretical knowledge and practical skills in electronics and circuit design. My academic journey focuses on understanding electrical systems, programming, and hands-on experimentation with circuits and hardware.
Experience.EducationText: As an electrical engineering student, I am building a strong foundation in circuit analysis, electronics, and programming. Through coursework and lab projects, I am gaining hands-on experience designing and testing circuits, as well as solving engineering problems using programming tools.
Experience.SkillsText: I have experience with programming languages such as C++ and Python, and basic web technologies like CSS. In electrical engineering, I have developed fundamental skills in circuit analysis and building circuits on breadboards. I enjoy applying these skills in practical projects and continuously improving my technical knowledge.

Project1.Title: Frequency Detector Sallen-Key Filter Project
Project1.Desc:
Designed and built a 4th-order Sallen-Key bandpass filter to detect a 10 kHz signal while attenuating unwanted frequencies such as 17 kHz and 25 kHz. The project involved circuit design, hand calculations, MATLAB analysis, LTspice simulation, and physical implementation using TL081 op-amps on a breadboard. The results from simulation and lab testing were compared to evaluate the filter performance.

It has a GitHub repo and more details shown in screenshots.
